title: Self-Esteem Self-Check
description: This brief self-check explores how you currently relate to yourself — your inner voice, your sense of worth, and how you respond to setbacks. It is not a diagnostic tool. Its purpose is to help you notice patterns of self-criticism and track how they change as you work through this course.
instructions: For each statement below, choose the response that best describes how often you have experienced this over the past 2 weeks. Answer honestly — there are no right or wrong answers.

questions.text: I speak to myself in a harsh or insulting way
When I make a mistake, I treat it as proof that I am a failure
I find it hard to notice my strengths or positive qualities
I feel unworthy of kindness, respect, or good things happening to me
I compare myself unfavorably to other people
I avoid situations where I might be judged or seen as inadequate
I dismiss or downplay my achievements and successes
I believe I am fundamentally flawed or defective as a person

bands.label: Low self-criticism
bands.description: Your responses suggest a relatively healthy relationship with yourself over the past two weeks. You may still experience occasional self-doubt — that is normal — but it does not appear to be a dominant pattern. The skills in this course can help you maintain and strengthen your self-worth.

bands.label: Moderate self-criticism
bands.description: You endorsed a moderate level of self-critical patterns. This is common and very workable. The strategies in this course — particularly self-compassion practices and cognitive restructuring — are well-suited to help you develop a kinder inner voice. Consider tracking your progress over time.

bands.label: Significant self-criticism
bands.description: Your answers suggest self-criticism is meaningfully affecting your daily life and well-being. We encourage you to work through this course AND consider speaking with a mental health professional. Therapy approaches like CBT and compassion-focused therapy have strong evidence for shifting these patterns.

bands.label: High self-criticism
bands.description: Your answers suggest a persistent and intense pattern of self-criticism that is likely causing significant distress. We strongly encourage you to seek professional support if you haven't already. A therapist experienced with self-esteem and self-compassion work can provide personalized guidance alongside what you learn in this course. If you are in crisis or feeling unsafe, please reach out to a crisis resource immediately.

functionalImpairmentQuestion.text: How much has self-criticism or low self-worth interfered with your daily life, relationships, or work over the past 2 weeks?

disclaimer: This self-check is for educational self-reflection only. It is NOT a diagnostic tool and cannot replace professional evaluation. Low self-esteem can co-occur with depression, anxiety, and other conditions that benefit from professional treatment. Do not use these results to self-diagnose. If you are experiencing thoughts of self-harm or suicide, contact the 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline by calling or texting 988.
